dialogue: ☘ Fun Fact: The first St. Patrick's Day parade in America was held in Boston in 1737 — over a century before Scranton's parade began!
☘ Fun Fact: The Brian P. Kelly Memorial Footrace has been a St. Patrick's Day morning tradition in Scranton since 1978. Runners wear green head-to-toe!
☘ Fun Fact: St. Peter's Cathedral was built by Irish immigrant stonemasons in 1880. They used Scranton's own Nay Aug brownstone!
☘ Fun Fact: The Irish worked as 'powder monkeys' in the coal mines — their job was to set the dynamite charges. Dangerous work!
☘ Fun Fact: The Electric City sign was nearly demolished in 1974! Local citizens rallied to save it. Today it's on the National Register of Historic Places.
☘ Fun Fact: The Scranton St. Patrick's Parade has run every year since 1962 — rain, snow, or shine. It draws over 20,000 spectators!
